events.title: Israel dismisses calls to add Lebanon to US-Iran ceasefire
events.description: Israel vowed more strikes against Hezbollah on Thursday, dismissing mounting international demands that the fragile truce between the United States and Iran in the Gulf be expanded to cover the war in Lebanon. At least 203 people were killed and 1,000 wounded in the latest strikes, the Lebanese health ministry said. FRANCE 24's Noga Tarnopolsky reports from Jerusalem.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel dismisses calls to add Lebanon to US-Iran ceasefire

events.title: Lebanon declares day of mourning after over 250 killed in Israeli attacks
events.description: Lebanon declared a national day of mourning on Thursday as Israel bombed more targets in the country, after its largest attacks of the war on Wednesday killed more than 250 people and injured over 1,000, threatening to derail Donald Trump’s truce from the outset.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon declares day of mourning after over 250 killed in Israeli attacks

events.title: Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month
events.description: The Middle East crisis has lurched into its second month, prompting UN Secretary-General António Guterres to issue a stark warning on Thursday morning that the world is “on the edge of a wider war” with catastrophic global implications.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Guterres warns of ‘wider war’ as Middle East conflict enters second month

events.title: Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China
events.description: Beijing’s powerbrokers are credited with winning Iran over, although one analyst says they were ‘pushing an open door’ Middle East crisis – live updates As the world struggles to make sense of what, if anything, was achieved by the ceasefire deal announced by the US and Iran on Tuesday, one major power that stands to win regardless is China. Beijing’s powerbrokers are being credited with pushing Iran towards agreeing to the ceasefire, bolstering its status as a regional mediator. In China’s tightly censored domestic media, articles basking in the glory of China being the grown-up in the room at a time of international crisis were allowed to circulate. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Who can claim victory if Iran ceasefire holds? An early winner is China

events.title: Taking stock of Iran war after 40 days of conflict
events.description: As global attention turns to the fragile ceasefire agreed between Iran and the United States, the 40 days of conflict have left lasting scars across Iran – with significant damage to infrastructure and military capabilities, even as Tehran claims victory. In Washington, the Trump administration has gone further, suggesting the Islamic Republic has undergone regime change, but on the ground, Iran’s leadership remains firmly in place. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Taking stock of Iran war after 40 days of conflict

events.title: UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant
events.description: Reports of yet another projectile strike near the Bushehr nuclear power plant prompted Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), to register his deep concern on Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN nuclear agency chief ‘deeply concerned’ by reports of latest attack on Iran power plant

events.title: 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official
events.description: One attacker is dead and two others injured after a gunfight with police outside the Israeli consulate in Istanbul, Turkey's interior minister says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 3 gunmen open fire outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul, dubbed 'terrorists' by Turkish official

events.title: India hit by Middle East war: Millions face gas shortages
events.description: In the weeks leading up to the ceasefire, the impact of the war in the Middle East was felt far beyond the region, reaching countries such as India. The nation of nearly 1.5 billion people relies heavily on imports of cooking gas, or LPG, much of it sourced from the Middle East. When Iran blocked the Strait of Hormuz – a critical global shipping route – supplies were severely disrupted. Long queues formed outside distribution centres in major cities, forcing many to turn to the black market. The conflict has also put at risk the lives of nearly ten million Indians living and working in Gulf countries. At least eight Indian migrant workers have been killed in war-related incidents. Many others remain stranded, unable to afford the high cost of flights home, as their families wait anxiously for their return. Even with a fragile ceasefire in place, those affected continue to live in precarity – and, for some, in grief. Report by Supriya Kumar, Fantine Dantzer, Alban Alvarez and Khansa Juned.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ India hit by Middle East war: Millions face gas shortages

events.title: At least 182 killed as Israel strikes central Beirut after saying Iran truce doesn't apply there
events.description: Lebanon’s health ministry says that Israeli strikes during the day killed 182 people, the highest single-day death toll in the Israel-Hezbollah war
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 182 killed as Israel strikes central Beirut after saying Iran truce doesn't apply there

events.title: Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal
events.description: Iran says the U.S. must choose between ceasefire or war via Israel as Tuesday's ceasefire deal excludes Tehran-backed terrorist group Hezbollah.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran threatens to end ceasefire over Hezbollah's exclusion from truce deal

events.title: Strait of Hormuz not open, Abu Dhabi’s oil chief says as crude prices rise
events.description: Uncertainty over US-Iran ceasefire pushes price of Brent crude towards $100 a barrel Business live – latest updates Middle East crisis – live updates The boss of Abu Dhabi’s state-owned oil company has said the strait of Hormuz is “not open” despite the US-Iran ceasefire agreed earlier this week, as uncertainty over the truce pushed the price of Brent crude towards $100 a barrel on Thursday. Sultan Al Jaber, the chief executive officer of the Abu Dhabi National Oil Company (Adnoc), said passage through the crucial waterway was subject to “permission, conditions and political leverage” by Iran. He said energy security and global economic stability depended on the strait being opened “fully, unconditionally and without restriction”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Strait of Hormuz not open, Abu Dhabi’s oil chief says as crude prices rise

events.title: Miner rescued alive after spending nearly 2 weeks trapped underground in flooded area
events.description: A Mexican miner was rescued after spending 13 days trapped underground, officials said, while one miner was found dead and another remains missing.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Miner rescued alive after spending nearly 2 weeks trapped underground in flooded area

events.title: #Lebanon: Scenes of destruction day after ‘terrifying’ Israeli #strikes
events.description: Lebanon declared a national day of mourning on Thursday, a day after Israel launched its biggest attack of the war on its neighbour so far, leaving widespread destruction in the capital Beirut and killing more than 250 people according to the country’s civil defence service.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 #Lebanon: Scenes of destruction day after ‘terrifying’ Israeli #strikes

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise
events.description: Strikes and counter-strikes continue across the Middle East, with dozens of casualties reported over the weekend in Lebanon following Israeli strikes targeting the south and the capital, Beirut. Meanwhile, humanitarian needs are rising, critical infrastructure remains under strain, and the wider economic and global impacts of the crisis continue to mount. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 6 April: Strikes persist across region as humanitarian needs rise

events.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
events.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes

events.title: Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms
events.description: President Trump said that any peace deal would not allow nuclear enrichment in Iran, and would need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, as conflicting messages surface over the terms of the ceasefire.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms

events.title: World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security
events.description: The UN is significantly scaling up its presence in the Sudanese capital, Khartoum, to expand life-saving operations as the conflict between rival militaries approaches its third year.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: ‘Skyrocketing’ needs outpace Sudan funding, Ukraine strikes update, global water security

events.title: Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies
events.description: The UN has issued an urgent call for international support as Cuba grapples with a ‘worsening’ humanitarian crisis fuelled by a prolonged energy blockade and the lingering devastation caused by Hurricane Melissa last year.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Cuba energy crisis: Humanitarian needs remain despite fuel supplies

events.title: Iran war: Catalyst for a renewable energy push?
events.description: The US and Israel’s war with Iran has triggered one of the biggest global energy shocks in decades. Asian economies, in particular, are facing disruptions to oil and gas supplies, while energy prices have surged worldwide. Climate activists say the shock should serve as a catalyst in the switch to renewable energy sources.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Catalyst for a renewable energy push?

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire
events.description: With a US-Iran ceasefire offering a fragile glimmer of hope after weeks of conflict, violence continues to reverberate across the Middle East. Massive airstrikes in Lebanon have caused heavy civilian casualties and widespread destruction, drawing strong UN condemnation. As humanitarian needs deepen and diplomatic efforts intensify, the situation remains highly volatile. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 9 April: UN condemns massive strikes in Lebanon, as concerns grow over ceasefire

events.title: Track current petrol and diesel prices, service station outages and shipments – Australia's fuel crisis in charts
events.description: How much fuel does Australia have left today, and when could we run out? Check how much petrol and diesel prices have risen near you in Sydney, Melbourne and across the country since the US and Israel’s war on Iran began in late February Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hundreds of service stations across Australia have run empty, fuel prices are elevated and oil shipments have been cancelled. Australia is battling a fuel crisis as Iran’s closure of the strait of Hormuz continues to bite. The federal government has released fuel reserves, cut fuel excise taxes and rolled out a national fuel security plan . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Track current petrol and diesel prices, service station outages and shipments – Australia's fuel crisis in charts

events.title: Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight
events.description: Baby was delivered during Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston to the US; nationality of child to be determined Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox A routine passenger flight from Jamaica landed at New York ’s John F Kennedy international airport with one more person than it took off with after a woman gave birth in midair, potentially setting up a tricky situation over the newborn’s citizenship. The “medical event” occurred on a Caribbean Airlines flight from Kingston on Saturday, according to a news release from the carrier. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Stork warning: woman gives birth midair on Jamaica-to-New York flight

events.title: Rescuers search for survivors in Lebanese capital amid new Israeli strikes
events.description: Rescuers searched for survivors under the rubble of destroyed buildings in Beirut on Thursday as Lebanon observed a day of mourning after Israeli strikes across the country killed more than 200. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Rescuers search for survivors in Lebanese capital amid new Israeli strikes

events.title: Diesel and jet fuel shortages for some time, IMF warns
events.description: The world will experience diesel and jet fuel shortages "for some time" because of the war in Iran, according to the International Monetary Fund (IMF).
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Diesel and jet fuel shortages for some time, IMF warns

events.title: Iranians rally to mark 40-day mourning for slain supreme leader Khamenei
events.description: Mourners across Iran began ceremonies Thursday marking the 40th day after the death of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed at the start of the Iran war. In Iran’s capital, Tehran, mourners wearing black began their rally from Jomhouri Eslami Square to the neighborhood of the office of Khamenei, 86.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iranians rally to mark 40-day mourning for slain supreme leader Khamenei

events.title: Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears
events.description: At least 326 humanitarians were killed in the line of duty across 21 countries during 2025, bringing the total killed over three years to over 1,010. The International Red Cross warned the Security Council on Wednesday that “we are losing our humanity in war.”
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Over 1,000 humanitarians have been killed in three years, Security Council hears

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon
events.description: After nearly 40 days of intense hostilities across the Middle East – marked by rising civilian casualties and widespread damage to critical infrastructure – a two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been announced. While the UN Secretary-General has welcomed the move as a step toward a broader peace, reports of continued Israeli strikes and mass casualties across Lebanon underscore the fragility of the situation. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 8 April: US-Iran ceasefire announced; strikes continue in Lebanon

More than 300 people – including firefighters, police and military personnel – are taking part in the search operation, an official from the Daejeon fire headquarters said. Continue reading...

events.title: Australian spy plane operators in Middle East not sharing intel with US for offensive operations, defence boss says

As the federal government extended the deployment of the E-7 Wedgetail aircraft on Thursday, the chief of Defence, Admiral David Johnston, said the crew were taking active steps to only contribute to defensive operations. events.title: Iran war: Israel says it will hold talks with Lebanon 'soon'

events.title: 'Trump abandoned us': ceasefire sparks anger, fear, division among Iranians
events.description: Iranians are divided between relief, disappointment, fear and anger after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire and possible peace plan between the USA and Israel on one side, and Iran on the other. The FRANCE 24 Observers team talked to several Iranians inside Iran to capture their responses and first-hand impressions of the ceasefire after five weeks of armed conflict in their country.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Trump abandoned us': ceasefire sparks anger, fear, division among Iranians

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.description: Hostilities continue across the Middle East, with ongoing strikes, rising civilian impacts and mounting pressure on critical infrastructure and humanitarian services. On the diplomatic track in New York, China and Russia vetoed a Security Council resolution put forward by Gulf States to ensure safety and security of ships in the Strait of Hormuz - find full coverage here. Meanwhile, displacement remains high, aid access is constrained. Human rights chief Volker Türk says President Trump’s threat to annihilate Iranian civilisation is “sickening”. Stay with us for live updates from across the UN system. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 7 April: Humanitarian and economic pressures grow; Russia and China veto resolution on Strait of Hormuz

events.title: France unveils rearmament boost to face Russia threat, global instability
events.description: France is to ramp up explosive drone stocks by up to 400 percent by 2030, draft legislation showed Wednesday, as the country seeks to accelerate rearmament to face an aggressive Russia. The military spending bill was presented after France's top commanders said the country must be ready in the next few years for a clash with Russia and that NATO should adapt to produce more weapons and faster.

events.title: Philadelphia parking garage collapse leaves 1 dead, 2 missing
events.description: A search and rescue operation is underway in Philadelphia following the partial collapse of a parking garage that left one dead and two unaccounted for.

events.title: Hit New Zealand comedy duo Flight of the Conchords reunion gigs sell out in minutes
events.description: Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement announce four shows at Wellington venue Meow Nui from next week – their first gigs since 2018 New Zealand’s self-described “fourth most popular guitar-based digi-bongo-a cappella-rap-funk-comedy-folk duo” Flight of the Conchords sold out their first shows in eight years in minutes this week, sparking a frenzy among fans. Bret McKenzie and Jemaine Clement formed the musical comedy act in 1998, soaring to worldwide fame off the back of their HBO comedy series of the same name with tunes including Business Time and Hiphopopotamus vs Rhymenoceros. Continue reading...

events.title: US seeks to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia despite new Costa Rica deal
events.description: Man born in El Salvador has been fighting removal to series of ‘third’ countries after mistaken deportation last year US government attorneys on Tuesday told a federal judge the Department of Homeland Security still intends to deport Kilmar Ábrego García to Liberia, despite a new agreement with Costa Rica to accept deportees who cannot legally be returned to their home countries. The Salvadorian national’s case has become a focal point in the immigration debate after he was mistakenly deported to El Salvador last year. Since his return, he has been fighting a second deportation to a series of African countries proposed by homeland security officials. Continue reading...

events.title: Uganda receives first US deportation flight under third-country agreement
events.description: Dozen people arrive under new deal but legal challenges expected with scheme criticised as ‘dehumanising process’ A flight carrying people being deported from the US has landed in Uganda, as Donald Trump’s administration pushes on with its strategy of expelling migrants to countries they have no ties to. The deported people would stay in the east African country as “a transition phase for potential onward transmission to other countries”, an unnamed senior Ugandan government official told Reuters. Continue reading...

events.title: Chile’s far-right government rips up plan for memorial at Pinochet torture site
events.description: New administration reverses expropriation of property founded by ex-Nazi Paul Schäfer, leaving victims in limbo With its Germanic crosses and colourful toy-town facades, the village square of the tiny Chilean settlement of Villa Baviera gives little indication of the horrors of its past. Until 1991, this cattle town of a few hundred people was a compound known as Colonia Dignidad. Its leader, Paul Schäfer, a former Nazi and weapons smuggler, bought a swathe of land in the valley in 1961, eventually holding as many as 300 people in a fenced enclave with minimal contact with the outside world. He sexually abused and even tortured the children in the camp. Continue reading...

events.title: People of Burkina Faso should forget about democracy, says military ruler

Traoré took power in a coup in September 2022 , toppling another junta that had taken power just nine months earlier. He has since stifled opposition and in January banned political parties outright. Continue reading...

events.title: First Nation asks court to block Alberta referendum on seceding from Canada
events.description: Sturgeon Lake Cree Nation has asked a court to halt the separatist push, arguing it would violate their treaty rights A First Nation in Alberta has said that a separatist push for the province to secede from Canada is “consummately irresponsible and dishonourable” and should be shut down, arguing in court that a proposed referendum would violate their treaty rights. A minority of residents of the oil-rich province have long argued that the province’s woes are due to the structure of payments to the federal government and a perceived inability to get their vast fossil fuel reserves to market. Continue reading...

events.title: Argentina approves Milei’s glacier mining bill amid environmental protests
events.description: Legislative change backed by libertarian president makes it easier to extract metals in frozen parts of the Andes Argentina’s congress has approved a bill promoted by the libertarian president, Javier Milei, that authorises mining in ecologically sensitive areas of glaciers and permafrost, outraging environmentalists. The amendment to the “glacier law”, which was already approved by the senate in February, would make it easier to mine for metals such as copper, lithium and silver in frozen parts of the Andes mountains. Continue reading...

events.title: Traffic falls on major Sydney and Melbourne roads as fuel crisis sees Australians cut back on driving
events.description: Exclusive: Trips on Sydney’s key thoroughfares have fallen by thousands per day, according to government data Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Road traffic is falling on Australia’s east coast as fuel prices bite, with most key Sydney highways recording 20% fewer weekend trips. The number of trips recorded on Sydney’s key thoroughfares has fallen by thousands of trips a day, according to New South Wales government data shared exclusively with Guardian Australia. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘Inherently dangerous and unnecessary’: NT buses will soon be patrolled by armed officers
events.description: First Nations lawyers and politicians warn the change will disproportionately affect Indigenous people, making them feel ‘less safe, rather than more safe’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Transit safety and public housing officers in the Northern Territory will soon be armed with guns, in what the territory’s First Nations legal service has labelled an “inherently dangerous and unnecessary” move that would “disproportionately impact Aboriginal Territorians”. The first of a new force of armed Police Public Safety Officers (PPSOs) will begin patrolling Darwin, Palmerston, Katherine and Alice Springs in June, after an 18-week training program. Legislation to create the new PPSOs was introduced by the NT government last year, in response to what they said was an increase in antisocial behaviour on public transport. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Inherently dangerous and unnecessary’: NT buses will soon be patrolled by armed officers

Viki Graham, 46, was refused bail and will spend at least two months in jail after she was charged with wounding the 63-year-old man while he lay in a bed at Sydney’s Royal Prince Alfred hospital. events.title: ‘We still deserve due process,’ says Cambodian man deported by US to Eswatini
events.description: Pheap Rom was one of 15 people sent to prison in African kingdom last year despite completing US sentences A Cambodian man deported by the US said he would have accepted being sent to Cambodia, but instead ended up imprisoned in Eswatini, a country he knew so little about that when he first read the name he thought it was another immigration detention centre in Louisiana. Pheap Rom, who had been convicted of attempted murder, was one of 10 deportees sent to Eswatini by the US in October 2025. They joined a group of five men, from Cambodia, Cuba, Jamaica, Vietnam and Yemen, who were deported to the small southern African country in July. All were sent to a maximum-security prison. Rom was deported from Eswatini to Cambodia in March. Continue reading...

events.title: Brazil bowler Cardoso takes 9 Lesotho wickets in record-breaking T20 win
events.description: Brazil's Laura Cardoso takes record nine-wicket haul in 189 win against Lesotho to claim best bowling figures in a T20I.

events.description: Tehran's plans to tax ships passing through the strait and raise money to rebuild is already seeing pushback.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: A day in the life of a 19-year-old in ICE detention: ‘I feel that this nightmare is not going to end’
events.description: Olivia has been detained for months at the sprawling Dilley center in Texas. She has lost 20lb, and wakes up every day with a headache Each day in detention feels like 48 hours for Olivia. The 19-year-old asylum seeker from the Democratic Republic of the Congo has been at the Dilley Immigration processing center in Texas for more than four months. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump meets with NATO chief about the future of the partnership
events.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday to discuss the future partnership after Trump disparaged the alliance.

events.title: Russia labels human rights group Memorial 'extremist'
events.description: Russia's Supreme Court deemed the storied rights organization Memorial "clearly anti-Russian" in a closed-door ruling. Memorial said the decision marked a new phase of repression in Russia.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Russia labels human rights group Memorial 'extremist'

events.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
events.description: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb

events.title: Southeast Asia trusts the EU more, but Brussels still has work to do
events.description: Southeast Asia increasingly sees the European Union as a trusted hedge against US-China rivalry, but analysts say Brussels will need stronger political engagement to turn growing goodwill into real strategic influence.

events.title: Germany: Fare evasion is a crime that can send you to prison
events.description: In Germany, riding without a ticket on public transportation can land you in jail. The justice minister would like to change this, but her conservative coalition partners are skeptical.

events.title: Migrant dinghy sinks in English Channel off France, 4 people die
events.description: A boat carrying more than 40 people ran into trouble early in the morning, with two men and two women dying in the waters. Crossings are building up after the winter lull, but the sea temperatures are slower to rise.

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
events.description: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

events.title: Hungarian election: How football has helped Orban keep power
events.description: Viktor Orban is a football fanatic but has long used the sport as a political tool, both in Hungary and abroad. With polls predicting election defeat, he could also lose his chance of hosting the Champions League final.

events.title: 🔴 Live: Israel's Netanyahu says instructed cabinet 'to begin direct negotiations' with Lebanon
events.description: No description available.

events.title: #Pope Leo XIV learns to spin #basketball with Harlem Globetrotters
events.description: Members of the US exhibition basketball team the Harlem Globetrotters taught Pope Leo XIV to spin a basketball on his finger on Wednesday as they met with the pontiff at his weekly general audience in Saint Peter's Square. The moment drew loud cheers from the watching crowds.

events.title: France's public prosecutor drops drugs probe into far-left MEP
events.description: The ⁠Paris Public ​Prosecutor's office said on Thursday it has stopped investigating French far-left member of the European Parliament, Rima Hassan, over illegal drug ​possession. Police last week found Hassan in possession of cannabidiol, or CBD, and a substance that seemed to be a designer drug 3-MMC.

events.title: Cannes Festival promises escapism in Hollywood-lite edition
events.description: The Cannes Film Festival unveiled its roster of films for this year's edition on Thursday packed with historical dramas, "gentleness" and even football, with the absence of major Hollywood studios set to dial down the American razzle-dazzle of previous editions. Festival director Thierry Fremaux unveiled 21 films in competition for the main Palme d'Or prize, featuring former winners, newcomers and strong showings from Spain and Japan. Film Critic Ben Croll tells us more.

events.title: Trump hits out at NATO after meeting alliance chief
events.description: Donald Trump has repeated his criticism of NATO after holding talks with the alliance's secretary general in Washington.

events.title: Trump's surprise claim in his call with Sky News
events.description: 👉 Follow Trump100 on your podcast app 👈

events.title: Argentina approves Milei's bill that eases protections for glaciers, despite environmental backlash
events.description: Argentina's Congress has approved a controversial bill easing glacier protections to boost mining investments

events.title: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney attracts yet another Conservative lawmaker to his Liberal Party
events.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has attracted another opposition Conservative lawmaker to the Liberal Party, further assuring that he will soon have a majority government

events.title: British pastor charged with manslaughter after man drowns at baptism
events.description: A British pastor faces manslaughter charges in the drowning of a 61-year-old man during a backyard baptism

events.title: Peruvians choosing a president from 35-candidate pool in Sunday's election
events.description: Peru's next president will be chosen from a pool of 35 candidates that includes a former minister, a comedian and a political dynasty heiress

events.title: Venezuelan acting President Delcy Rodríguez asks workers for patience and promises May wage increase
events.description: Venezuelan acting President Delcy Rodríguez has asked public and private sector workers for patience as her government works to improve the economy

events.title: Activists ring alarm bells about halt in Poland's air pollution progress
events.description: Poland's "Clean Air" program, aimed at reducing pollution and household heating demand, is losing momentum

events.title: Legendary mountaineer Jim Whittaker, first American to summit Mount Everest, dead at 97
events.description: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest in 1963, has died at 97, his family said, leaving a legacy in climbing and conservation.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Legendary mountaineer Jim Whittaker, first American to summit Mount Everest, dead at 97

events.title: Oklahoma principal shot in leg is praised for tackling school shooter: 'He is a hero'
events.description: Pauls Valley High School Principal Kirk Moore tackled a 20-year-old former student armed with a gun, officials say, preventing a worse school tragedy in Oklahoma.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Oklahoma principal shot in leg is praised for tackling school shooter: 'He is a hero'

events.title: Husband taken into custody in connection with disappearance of American woman and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: Wisconsin couple allegedly starved six children for years, forcing them to eat mold, bugs and dog food
events.description: Casey and Mary Cano face child abuse charges after allegedly starving their six children and beating them with belts at their Wisconsin home for years.

events.title: College student accused of letting newborn drown – police reveal phone photos of ‘hated mom’ Casey Anthony
events.description: A grand jury indicted Florida college student Anne Mae Demegillo on first-degree murder after she allegedly watched her newborn drown in a toilet at home.

events.title: Iran's UN ambassador takes swipe at Trump in final hours before Strait of Hormuz deadline
events.description: Iran's UN ambassador is calling one of President Donald Trump's posts on Truth Social on Tuesday “deeply irresponsible" and “profoundly alarming."
